originalTitle: Malawians repatriated from South Africa amid xenophobia concerns
title: Friends from Malawi Are Coming Home Safely!
content:
Hello from Africa!

Hello, amazing young reporters! I'm your friendly news reporter, and I have an important story to share with you from our wonderful continent, Africa. It's about some friends from a country called Malawi who are coming back home. Guess what? Many people from Malawi were living in another country called South Africa. But now, they are on their way back to Malawi. Their government is sending special buses to bring them home safely.

Why Are They Coming Home?

Well, sometimes, people need to move to other places to find work or to have new adventures. These Malawian grown-ups went to South Africa to find jobs. They wanted to earn money to help their families, buy food, clothes, and send their children to school. But sadly, some people in South Africa were not very kind to people who came from other countries, like Malawi. Imagine if someone was unkind to you just because you came from a different town or had a different accent. That's a bit like what was happening. When people are unkind or unfriendly to others just because they come from a different place, it makes everyone feel sad and scared. It's not a nice feeling at all. Did you know? We should always be kind to new friends, no matter where they come from! Kindness makes the world a better place.

Where is Malawi?

Malawi is a beautiful country right here in Africa! It's in the southeastern part of our continent. It has lovely green hills, big lakes, and many friendly people. Malawi is often called 'The Warm Heart of Africa.' This is because the people there are known for being very welcoming and kind. Isn't that a lovely nickname? Imagine your own town or village, full of people you know and love. That's what Malawi is like for these people. It's their home, where their families and friends are. Did you know? Lake Malawi is one of the largest lakes in Africa! It's so big, it looks like an ocean!

Where is South Africa?

South Africa is another big country, also in Africa. It's at the very bottom, or the 'south' part, of our continent. It's a very big country with tall buildings, busy cities, and amazing wildlife. In South Africa, you can see incredible animals like lions, elephants, giraffes, and zebras! It's famous for its beautiful natural parks. Many people live and work in South Africa, and it's a place where people from all over Africa, and the world, have come to live. Did you know? South Africa has 11 official languages! That's a lot of different ways to say 'hello'!

How Are They Coming Home?

Because it wasn't safe for the Malawian people in South Africa anymore, the government of Malawi said, "Come home, our dear friends and family! We will help you." So, they sent big, comfortable buses all the way to South Africa. These buses are making a very long trip to bring all the Malawian people back to their homes and families. Imagine a huge school bus, but even bigger, making a very special journey just to keep people safe. It’s a bit like when your parents come to pick you up when you feel scared. Many hundreds of people are getting on these buses. They are excited to see their families but might also feel a little sad to leave South Africa.

Other Countries Are Helping Too!

Malawi is not the only country in Africa that is helping its citizens. Other countries are also looking after their people who were living in South Africa. They all want to make sure their citizens are safe and sound. It shows how African countries care for each other and their people. It's like a big family helping each other out.

What Can We Learn?

This story reminds us of something super important: we should always be kind to everyone, no matter where they come from. It doesn't matter if someone has a different skin colour, speaks a different language, or comes from a different country. Everyone is special and deserves to be treated with love and respect. Imagine if a new kid comes to your school from another village or another country. It's important to say hello, invite them to play, and make them feel welcome. Being kind is like giving a warm hug to someone's heart. It makes everyone feel good, both the person giving it and the person receiving it! Let's all try to be the warmest hearts of Africa, just like Malawi.

A Happy Return

Now, these Malawian friends are coming home to their families. They will be so happy to be safe with their loved ones. Let's send them warm thoughts and hope they settle back into their homes happily. And let's all remember to be kind to everyone we meet, everywhere we go. Because everyone, absolutely everyone, deserves to feel safe and loved. Thanks for listening, amazing kid reporters!
summary: Many people from Malawi are coming home from South Africa because it was not safe for them there, and their government is bringing them back.
whyMatters: It's important to remember that everyone deserves to be safe and treated with kindness, no matter where they are from, and governments work hard to protect their people.

originalTitle: Somali referee Artan to miss World Cup after being barred from entering US
title: A Big Dream for Our African Referee, Omar Artan!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends all across Africa! Your kid-friendly news reporter is here with a story about a very special person. It's about dreams, hard work, and a little bit of sad news, but also lots of hope! Today, we are talking about a wonderful man named Omar Artan. He is from a beautiful country in Africa called Somalia. Can you say Somalia? It's a place with sunny skies and lovely people, just like you!

Meet Omar Artan: Our Whistle-Blowing Hero!

Omar Artan is a very important person in the world of football. Do you know what football is? It's that super fun game where players kick a ball into a net! Lots of kids love to play football, right? Well, Omar doesn't play the game with the ball, but he helps make sure everyone plays fair. Omar is a referee! A referee is like a special judge on the football field. He wears a special uniform and has a whistle. When someone breaks a rule, he blows his whistle! He makes sure all the players follow the rules, so the game is fair and fun for everyone. It's a very important job, and it takes a lot of practice and knowing all the rules. Did you know? A football game usually has 22 players, 11 on each team! The referee makes sure all 22 players play nicely together. Omar has worked very, very hard to be a referee. He has blown his whistle in many, many games, always making sure things are fair. Because he is so good at his job, he was chosen for something truly amazing!

The Biggest Football Party: The World Cup!

Imagine the biggest, most exciting football party in the whole wide world! That's what the World Cup is! Teams from all different countries, big and small, come together to play football. They wear colourful uniforms and try their best to win a shiny trophy. It's a huge celebration of football, and everyone watches it! Omar Artan was chosen to be a referee at this super special World Cup! Can you believe it? Out of so many referees in the world, Omar was picked! This was very exciting news for him and for everyone in Somalia and all of Africa. He was going to travel very far to a country called America to help with the games. Did you know? The World Cup happens only every four years! It's like a very special birthday that doesn't come around very often. That makes it even more exciting! What made it even *more* special is that Omar was going to be the *very first* referee from Somalia to ever go to the World Cup! Wow! This means he was making history! Everyone in Somalia was so proud, and all of Africa was cheering for him. He was going to show the world how amazing African talent is. He had his special uniform ready. He practiced blowing his whistle. He dreamed of being on that big field, helping the players. It was a dream come true for Omar, and a moment of great pride for his country.

A Little Problem on the Way

But then, something a little sad happened. To travel to a faraway country like America, you need special permission. It's like needing a special ticket to get into a big amusement park. This special ticket is called a 'visa' or travel papers. Omar needed this special paper to fly all the way to America and be part of the World Cup. Omar tried to get his special travel paper. He filled out all the forms and waited. But, sadly, he was told he couldn't get the special paper this time. This means he was 'denied entry' to America. It's like when you have a ticket for a party, but then something happens and you can't go. It's a bit disappointing, isn't it? Because he couldn't get the special travel paper, Omar Artan will not be able to go to the World Cup this year. He won't be able to blow his whistle on the big football fields in America. It's very sad news, especially because he worked so, so hard and was making history for Somalia and Africa.

How Do We Feel About It?

When we hear news like this, it can make us feel a little sad for Omar. He had such a big dream, and he was so close to making it come true. But even though he can't go this time, we are still super, super proud of him! Omar Artan is still a hero. He showed everyone what amazing things people from Africa can do. He worked hard, he dreamed big, and he got chosen for the biggest football event in the world! That is something truly special and wonderful. Did you know? Even when things don't go as planned, it's important to remember how hard you tried. Trying your best is always something to be proud of! We send Omar Artan big cheers and lots of love from all of Africa. We know he will keep being a fantastic referee, and maybe next time, he will get to go to the World Cup.

What Happens Next?

While Omar won't be there, another referee will take his place to help with the World Cup games. But Omar will keep practicing and keep blowing his whistle in other games. He will keep helping football players play fair. This story teaches us something very important: always try your best! Even if something doesn't work out exactly as you planned, you should never give up on your dreams. Omar Artan is an example of someone who worked very hard to achieve his dream, and even though he faced a challenge, he is still a champion in our eyes. Let's all cheer for Omar Artan, our African hero! And let's keep cheering for all the amazing people from Africa who work hard and make us proud. Keep dreaming big, my friends, and never stop trying! You can do amazing things too!
summary: Omar Artan, a talented referee from Somalia, won't be able to go to the World Cup in America because he couldn't get special travel papers, but we are still very proud of his hard work and big dream.
whyMatters: This story matters because Omar Artan is an African hero who worked very hard to achieve a big dream, showing us that even if there are problems, we should always be proud of our efforts and never give up on what we want to do.

title: Why Olise can be one of France's very best - Clichy
content: Former France international Gael Clichy explains why Bayern Munich's France forward Michael Olise could be the breakout star of this summer's World Cup.
